description: Hub route map for SSA → Suka Village Family Fund Hub data transmission. SSA owns the data first; Hub is an optional upstream. These routes are used by FamilyFundConnector.js and any other hub-aware modules to decide WHERE to send each kind of packet.

ssa.event.notes: Generic SSA event fallback – use when the event kind is unknown or not explicitly mapped.

import.parsed.notes: Normalized imports from external sites (recipe, cleaning, garden/seed, animal/butchery, storehouse, video/how-to).

inventory.updated.notes: Inventory changes that the Hub may aggregate for community-level storehouse views.

inventory.shortage.detected.notes: Demand signal – Hub businesses can respond with supply.

storehouse.low.notes: Storehouse goals not met; Hub can match to pantry / long-term supply.

meal.executed.notes: Meals cooked/eaten – Hub can derive consumption patterns.

garden.harvest.logged.notes: Produce available from households – Hub can route to barter / micro-markets.

preservation.completed.notes: Preserved goods – Hub can track community storage capacity.

commerce.offers.generated.notes: Commerce engine found products to satisfy demand – Hub can index offers.

commerce.barter.suggested.notes: Barter suggestions emitted by SSA – Hub can match across households.

analytics.waste.logged.notes: Waste events – Hub can detect community-level leakages.

analytics.preservation.method.updated.notes: Preservation stats – helps Hub understand which methods households use.

ssa.batch.notes: Batch packet containing multiple SSA events.

notes: 1. SSA MUST still function when these routes are unreachable.
2. FamilyFundConnector.js should consult this file when deciding where to POST.
3. When a kind is missing, use routes['ssa.event'] as the fallback.
4. These routes may be overridden at runtime by window.__suka.hub.routes.
5. Use this map for future domains too: preservation, animal, storehouse, construction.
